label: Bayesian & approximate inference

node.label: Bayesian & approximate inference
node.definition: Posterior beliefs over hidden variables or parameters are estimated exactly or approximately.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Bayesian & approximate inference is to connect Probability theory, Statistics & experimental design to downstream capabilities including MCMC, EM & probabilistic message passing, k-NN & Naive Bayes, Gaussian processes.
examples: substrate for MCMC, EM & probabilistic message passing
limitations: A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Probability theory, Statistics & experimental design.

dependencies.rationale: Bayesian & approximate inference uses Probability theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Bayesian & approximate inference uses Statistics & experimental design as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Bayesian & approximate inference uses Latent variables & hidden state as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Bayesian & approximate inference uses Numerical analysis as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
